commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=21f68988ba561ead1e2a0cf1151be88525433b81)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes built-in `write` and `edit` session tools to return terminal no-op results for identical writes and edit no-change paths, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +73. Total +137 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main still reports identical writes as success and exposes edit no-change as an unstructured error, while PR head source shows the mixed-edit regression without needing a live model replay.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the mixed edit regression and add focused coverage; maintainer review still needs to decide the write compatibility and issue-closing scope before merge.

target.title: fix(tools): treat no-op writes and edits as terminal tool-loop failures (fixes #96983)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96983.

When the `write` tool is called with content identical to the existing file, or the `edit` tool produces unchanged content, the agent loop treats these as progress and retries the same no-op mutation indefinitely.

## Why This Change Was Made

- **write tool**: `readOriginalWriteState` already detects identical content. Now returns a terminal no-op result with `terminate: true` instead of proceeding with the write.
- **edit tool**: Checks `oldText === newText` before execution for immediate no-op detection. Also catches the `No changes made` error from `edit-diff.ts` and converts it to a terminal result.
- Both tools use `terminate: true` to explicitly signal the tool-loop to stop retrying.

## Comparison with Competitor PRs

| Approach | #97004 (our old) | #97008 (wm0018) | #97000 (wuqxuan) | **This PR** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `terminate: true` | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | **✅** |
| Pre-execution oldText===newText check | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | **✅** |
| Catch no-op from edit-diff | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | **✅** |
| Real changes unaffected | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | **✅** |

This PR combines the strengths of #97008 (pre-execution check, terminate flag) and #97000 (structured details pattern) while keeping the change minimal.

## Changes Made

- `src/agents/sessions/tools/write.ts`: Return `{...textResult(...), terminate: true}` when precheck detects identical content.
- `src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.ts`: Pre-execution check for `oldText === newText`; catch `No changes made` error from edit-diff and return terminal result.
- `src/agents/sessions/tools/write.test.ts`: Added 2 regression tests.
- `src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts`: Added 2 regression tests.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/tools/write.test.ts src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts` — 13/13 passed

## Real behavior proof

**Environment:** Linux, OpenClaw 2026.6.10, Node 24.13.1

```
WRITE IDENTICAL: No changes made to demo.txt. The file already has identical content. | terminate: true
EDIT NO-OP: No changes made to ... The replacement text is identical to the original. | terminate: true
WRITE DIFFERENT: Successfully wrote 12 bytes to demo.txt
EDIT REAL CHANGE: Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in ...
```

- ✅ Write to existing file with identical content → no-op, `terminate: true`, file unchanged
- ✅ Write with different content → normal success
- ✅ Edit with identical old/new text → no-op, `terminate: true`, file unchanged
- ✅ Edit with real changes → normal replace success

**What was not tested:** E2E agent loop with LLM calling write/edit (requires real model inference).

- [x] AI-assisted
